ead.archdesc:
======= RMM07874William Levy collection on Suck magazine and Wet Dream Film Festival7874Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections2B Carl A. Kroch LibraryCornell UniversityIthaca, NY 14853rareref@cornell.eduURL: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 19:06:20 UTC. >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
William Levy collection on Suck magazine and Wet Dream Film Festival
Levy, William
7874
.3 cubic feet.
1 mapcase folders.
1965-1999 1965-1974 Correspondence from Germaine Greer, Maurice Girodias, Grshon Legman, Jean-Jacques Lebel, and the editors of Oz and Screw magazines, and others; notes and documents, including several manuscripts by Greer; and posters and printed ephemera relating to the production of Suck magazine and the Wet Dream Film Festivals. 11064306
Content Description
Correspondence from Germaine Greer, Maurice Girodias, Grshon Legman, Jean-Jacques Lebel, and the editors of Oz and Screw magazines, and others; notes and documents, including several manuscripts by Greer; and posters and printed ephemera relating to the production of Suck magazine and the Wet Dream Film Festivals.
Biographical / Historical
Levy and other co-editors of Suck magazine, Europe's first sexpaper, orgainzed two erotic film festivals in Amsterdam in 1970 and 1971. Called Wet Dream Film Festivals, they were the first of their kind. Suck rebelled against sexual repression in all its forms, promoted sexual freedom and experimentation, and helped pave the way for the personal and political liberation movements that followed.
Preferred Citation
William Levy collection on Suck magazine and Wet Dream Film Festival, #7874. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Suck magazine Oz magazine Screw magazine Wet Dream Film Festival -- Amsterdam Sexual freedom. Erotic films. Heterosexuality. Greer, Germaine, 1939- Blight, Rose, 1939- Girodias, Maurice. Legman, Grshon Lebel, Jean-Jacques
